sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a clear research vision (g.1.a) transitioning from patient experience to independent research leadership. It presents distinctive ideas (g.1.b) regarding health literacy in care workers and AI-driven discharge summaries. The training plan (g.1.c) is comprehensive, mapping specific courses to work packages. Mentorship (g.1.d) is robust, with named supervisors, defined meeting frequencies, and institutional support from UCL IHI.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats or missing details identified; the application fully meets the criteria for common characteristics of good applications.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats or missing details identified; the application fully meets the criteria for common characteristics of good applications.

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant explicitly articulates why this award is the right next step (g.2.a), moving from an ACF to a PhD to build independence. The difference the award makes (g.2.b) is clear: enabling a career in academia and bridging health/social care. It builds on existing experience (g.2.c) by leveraging prior health literacy work and adding AI/qualitative skills. Partnerships (g.2.d) are well-developed through Vivaldi, HCIC, and NHS trusts.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the justification for the award is specific and directly linked to career progression.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the justification for the award is specific and directly linked to career progression.

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: Strong evidence of previous projects (g.3.a) including a PI-led cross-sectional survey on health literacy. Formal training (g.3.b) is evidenced by an MPH with Distinction, MFPH, and specific courses in R and systematic reviews. Alignment (g.3.c) is perfect, as prior experience in health literacy and survey design directly prepares the applicant for the proposed study.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks; the applicant's background is highly relevant and well-documented.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks; the applicant's background is highly relevant and well-documented.

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant lists multiple high-quality publications (g.4.a) in journals like Anaesthesia and British Journal of Surgery. Awards (g.4.c) include the Doubleday Student Award and Patient and Public Involvement Award. Practical impact (g.4.d) is demonstrated by findings leading to changes in appointment letters and mandatory training. Citation metrics (g.4.b) are not explicitly quantified in the text, though the quality of journals implies impact.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While publication quality is high, specific citation counts or impact factors are not explicitly listed in the text, preventing a perfect score for g.4.b.
sub_criteria.rationale: While publication quality is high, specific citation counts or impact factors are not explicitly listed in the text, preventing a perfect score for g.4.b.

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: Clear career ambitions (g.5.a) to become a public health academic. Increasing responsibility (g.5.b) is shown through roles as PI, Visiting Lecturer, and conference organizer. Participation in leadership programs (g.5.c) includes facilitator training and executive coaching. Recognition (g.5.d) is evidenced by multiple awards and being a trustee for the Doubleday Centre.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks; the trajectory towards research leadership is clearly evidenced and supported by multiple indicators.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks; the trajectory towards research leadership is clearly evidenced and supported by multiple indicators.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: Explicit statement (g.6.a) of a practitioner-academic career path via the NIHR Academic Clinical Fellowship. Involvement in clinical research (g.6.b) is demonstrated by conducting studies while training as a junior doctor. Balancing practice (g.6.c) is shown by completing research in spare time and during fellowship. Professional qualifications (g.6.d) include MBChB, MFPH, and active roles in medical education.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks; the commitment to a practitioner-academic career is explicit and well-supported by current roles and past experience.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks; the commitment to a practitioner-academic career is explicit and well-supported by current roles and past experience.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is exceptionally clear, with NLP metrics confirming high readability (Flesch-Kincaid 6.86) and low jargon density (1.03%). It comprehensively covers the problem, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and impact in accessible language, aligning perfectly with the detailed research plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary is perfectly executed for a non-specialist audience.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats or missing details found; the summary is perfectly executed for a non-specialist audience.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a robust evidence review citing 24 references, critically analyzing gaps in health literacy literature and MDS readability. It logically connects existing evidence to the specific knowledge gaps this study aims to fill.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the review is comprehensive and well-integrated into the proposal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the review is comprehensive and well-integrated into the proposal.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The project scope is well-aligned with a doctoral fellowship, featuring four distinct work packages (survey, mixed methods, Delphi, AI feasibility) that are feasible within the 36-month timeline. Sample sizes and recruitment strategies are clearly defined.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the scope is appropriately ambitious yet realistic for the award level.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the scope is appropriately ambitious yet realistic for the award level.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described with high rigor, including PRISMA guidelines for systematic reviews, validated tools (FHLT, PEMAT) for surveys and document analysis, and a modified Delphi method for consensus. Sample size calculations and analysis plans (R, NVivo) are detailed.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the methodological design is robust and logically aligned with aims.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the methodological design is robust and logically aligned with aims.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly articulates direct benefits to patients (safety, reduced readmissions) and the NHS (cost savings, reduced workload). It aligns with societal needs regarding the aging population and health inequalities in social care.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; impact is specific and well-justified.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; impact is specific and well-justified.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Engagement is deeply integrated, with the research question co-produced by care workers and residents. The methodology includes a steering committee with lived experience experts, focus groups, and adaptations (e.g., printed materials) based on public feedback.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; engagement is meaningful and spans all project stages.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; engagement is meaningful and spans all project stages.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: Resources are explicitly linked to tasks (e.g., NLP developer for WP4, travel for data collection). The budget is proportional to the scope, with detailed justifications for staff, equipment, and participant incentives.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the budget is well-justified and comprehensive.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the budget is well-justified and comprehensive.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence provided in the 'Proposed Research' section regarding the applicant's previous research outputs or specific prior projects relevant to this proposal. This information is typically found in the Applicant CV or Research Background sections, which are outside the scope of this specific rubric section scoring.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The 'Proposed Research' section does not contain evidence of the applicant's prior research quality or outputs. While the applicant likely has a strong background (as seen in other sections), this specific section does not demonstrate it.
sub_criteria.rationale: The 'Proposed Research' section does not contain evidence of the applicant's prior research quality or outputs. While the applicant likely has a strong background (as seen in other sections), this specific section does not demonstrate it.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The design explicitly addresses inclusivity through purposive sampling for diversity, compensation for participants, and adaptive methods (printed materials, face-to-face options) to overcome digital barriers for older adults. Budget lines support these accessibility measures.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; inclusivity is well-considered and resourced.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; inclusivity is well-considered and resourced.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a highly detailed and structured training plan (sectdars__001_a-d) that explicitly maps specific courses (e.g., NVivo, R, Plain English Diploma, Facilitator skills) to each work package (WP1-WP4). The budget section (secsb__001_i) corroborates this with specific line items for these courses, ensuring completeness and clarity. The plan covers the full spectrum of required skills from systematic reviews to AI and dissemination.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan is comprehensive, the sequencing for the AI training (WP4) is noted as dependent on the 'version of the model' and 'best course available' (sectdars__001_c), indicating a slight lack of fixed timeline certainty compared to the other modules, preventing a perfect score on timeline rigidity.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan is comprehensive, the sequencing for the AI training (WP4) is noted as dependent on the 'version of the model' and 'best course available' (sectdars__001_c), indicating a slight lack of fixed timeline certainty compared to the other modules, preventing a perfect score on timeline rigidity.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant clearly identifies specific skill gaps (e.g., needing to 'refresh' systematic review knowledge, needing 'further' skills in R data visualization, needing 'better understanding' of AI models) and maps targeted training directly to these gaps (sectdars__001_a-b). The plan is highly targeted rather than generic, citing specific providers (UCL DSDP, SRA, Plain English Campaign) and linking them to project tasks like chairing the steering committee (facilitator course) and qualitative analysis (NVivo).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks found; the identification of gaps and the mapping to specific, relevant training activities are explicit and well-evidenced throughout the text.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks found; the identification of gaps and the mapping to specific, relevant training activities are explicit and well-evidenced throughout the text.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly states the goal of developing skills for a 'future academic career' (sectdars__001_a) and details training in grant writing, leadership, and dissemination (sectdars__001_d) that are transferable beyond the PhD. It includes specific professional development elements like executive coaching (Dave Thornton) and facilitator skills (sectdars__001_c, sectdars__001_e) that support long-term career aspirations in public health and academia.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material drawbacks found; the connection between the training plan and long-term career trajectory is robust and clearly articulated.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material drawbacks found; the connection between the training plan and long-term career trajectory is robust and clearly articulated.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides strong evidence of the contracting organisation's (UCL IHI) track record. It details the Vivaldi study led by the primary supervisor, the institute's history of supporting 400+ postgraduate students, and specific examples of ECR progression to funded posts (sectdars__001_q). It also highlights successful collaborations with NHS trusts and social care partners (sectdars__001_l).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the track record is extensive, specific citation metrics or a list of recent high-impact publications directly from the institute (beyond the Vivaldi study) are not explicitly enumerated in the text, though the general reputation is well-supported.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the track record is extensive, specific citation metrics or a list of recent high-impact publications directly from the institute (beyond the Vivaldi study) are not explicitly enumerated in the text, though the general reputation is well-supported.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The application details a robust supervisory team with four named supervisors, each with specific expertise aligned to the project's work packages (Public Health, Qualitative Methods, NLP/AI, Health Literacy). It explicitly defines meeting frequencies (fortnightly/monthly) and roles (sectdars__001_f, sectdars__001_y). It also includes a dedicated executive coach and a mentor for care workers on the steering committee, demonstrating a comprehensive mentorship plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the supervision plan is exceptionally detailed and well-aligned with project needs.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the supervision plan is exceptionally detailed and well-aligned with project needs.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly outlines the infrastructure provided by UCL IHI, including access to the Vivaldi network, NHS trust links for data access, and specific equipment (laptops, software) budgeted for the project. It explicitly links these resources to project success (e.g., NLP team support for WP4) and career growth (e.g., UCL Excellence Fellowships, Adapt to Thrive programme).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the support is comprehensive and directly tied to the project's specific requirements.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the support is comprehensive and directly tied to the project's specific requirements.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: Strong evidence of an inclusive culture is provided through mentions of UCL's Silver Athena Swan and Race Equality Charter awards. The text details an active EDI committee, diverse senior leadership, and specific initiatives to support underrepresented groups and rotate leadership roles. It also highlights the supervisor's personal commitment to EDI.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While policies and awards are cited, specific examples of how research integrity principles are enforced or monitored within the specific project context are less detailed than the EDI evidence, though the general institutional framework is strong.
sub_criteria.rationale: While policies and awards are cited, specific examples of how research integrity principles are enforced or monitored within the specific project context are less detailed than the EDI evidence, though the general institutional framework is strong.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides exceptional evidence of co-design. The research question was generated by a care worker during shadowing (secppi__001_a). Specific design changes were made based on community feedback, such as adapting the Delphi method to include printed versions and face-to-face discussions for older adults to overcome digital barriers (secppi__001_b). A steering committee with residents and families is established to guide the project (secsa__001_c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the integration of lived experience is comprehensive, specific, and directly evidenced across the proposal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the integration of lived experience is comprehensive, specific, and directly evidenced across the proposal.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The research team includes a diverse mix of experts by experience (care workers, residents, families) alongside academic and clinical experts (sectdars__001_f). The steering committee is explicitly designed to include these groups (secppi__001_c). The host organization highlights an ethnically diverse senior leadership team (sectdars__001_s).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the inclusion of lived experience is strong, explicit demographic data (e.g., specific ethnicity, gender breakdown) of the core research team members is not detailed, limiting the score for wp.2.a. Task allocation is described generally but not in granular detail for every team member.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the inclusion of lived experience is strong, explicit demographic data (e.g., specific ethnicity, gender breakdown) of the core research team members is not detailed, limiting the score for wp.2.a. Task allocation is described generally but not in granular detail for every team member.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: The application details a robust dissemination plan including scientific journals, conferences, and a dedicated knowledge share event co-led with care workers (secdrp__001_u). Clear links are drawn between the co-produced principles and anticipated benefits like reduced hospital readmissions and improved patient safety (secsa__001_c). The plan emphasizes actionable outcomes, such as a 'best practice guide' and AI tools for lay summaries (secpesor__001_c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the plan for maximizing public benefit is specific, actionable, and well-integrated into the project design.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the plan for maximizing public benefit is specific, actionable, and well-integrated into the project design.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The proposal explicitly targets care home residents and staff, groups often underrepresented in research (sectdars__001_l). Recruitment strategies include purposive sampling to maximize diversity in health literacy, care home size, and region (secdrp__001_p). Barriers to participation are addressed through specific mitigation strategies like travel costs and flexible meeting formats (secdrp__001_q).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the focus on care home residents is strong, the application could provide more specific detail on how other dimensions of diversity (e.g., ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation) will be actively recruited or represented beyond general purposive sampling.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the focus on care home residents is strong, the application could provide more specific detail on how other dimensions of diversity (e.g., ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation) will be actively recruited or represented beyond general purposive sampling.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the lifecycle: from co-designing the question (secppi__001_a) to adaptive methods in data collection (printed materials, face-to-face options for those with digital barriers) (secppi__001_b). Accessibility is prioritized in dissemination with plain English documents and technical accessories for steering committee members (secppi__001_c).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While plans for evaluation and adaptation are present (e.g., risk register, quarterly reviews), explicit documentation of a formal mechanism for ongoing evaluation of inclusivity metrics specifically is less detailed than the operational inclusion strategies.
sub_criteria.rationale: While plans for evaluation and adaptation are present (e.g., risk register, quarterly reviews), explicit documentation of a formal mechanism for ongoing evaluation of inclusivity metrics specifically is less detailed than the operational inclusion strategies.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The budget explicitly includes line items for PPIE involvement, participant vouchers (£15/£25), travel costs, and technical accessories (webcams, large print keyboards) for steering committee members (secsb__001_f, secsb__001_g, secsb__001_d). Staff time is allocated for training and coaching care workers (secsb__001_h). The resources align perfectly with the inclusion activities described in the research plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats; the costed plan is comprehensive, specific, and fully aligned with the inclusion strategy.
sub_criteria.rationale: No material caveats; the costed plan is comprehensive, specific, and fully aligned with the inclusion strategy.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates excellent structural organization with 10 detected parser sections, 26 numbered heading-like lines, and 34 list markers, ensuring clear navigation. A substantial word count across sections (e.g., 5140 words in Research Plan) indicates comprehensive content. The analysis confirms the presence of 17 extracted emphasis markers, supporting the use of bolding for key terms.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While emphasis markers are detected, the analysis notes a parser limitation where bold/emphasis may be lost during text extraction; therefore, the score for emphasis usage is slightly reduced to account for the inability to fully verify the visual hierarchy of bolded text in the final extracted format.
sub_criteria.rationale: While emphasis markers are detected, the analysis notes a parser limitation where bold/emphasis may be lost during text extraction; therefore, the score for emphasis usage is slightly reduced to account for the inability to fully verify the visual hierarchy of bolded text in the final extracted format.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: The application exhibits very low duplication with a duplicate sentence rate of 0.003 and a repeated line rate of 0.067, indicating high novelty and minimal redundancy. Lexical overlap between sections (e.g., 0.257 between Research Plan and Training) is moderate and likely reflects necessary thematic alignment rather than unnecessary repetition.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While duplication is low, the lexical overlap between the Detailed Research Plan and Training sections (0.257) is the highest among section pairs, suggesting some content may be repeated to ensure alignment, which prevents a perfect score for novelty in every section.
sub_criteria.rationale: While duplication is low, the lexical overlap between the Detailed Research Plan and Training sections (0.257) is the highest among section pairs, suggesting some content may be repeated to ensure alignment, which prevents a perfect score for novelty in every section.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates strong logical flow with 32 transition phrases and 95 objective-method-budget link terms, ensuring a coherent narrative thread. The section order follows a logical progression from applicant context to research plan, PPI, training, and budget. High lexical overlap between related sections (e.g., Research Plan and Training) supports semantic coherence and alignment of objectives.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No material caveats or missing details regarding logical flow were identified in the structural analysis; the evidence for coherence is robust and complete.
